title: Parity waiver / suppression (Phase 3a)
description: A waiver records ACCEPTED drift. CORE RULE (SEMANTICS.md sec 8): a waiver NEVER flips a dimension/finding to `pass`. The underlying result_state stays fail/unknown; the certificate records the finding as `waived`. Suppression only changes how the result is PRESENTED/gated, never the measured truth. An expired waiver stops suppressing. Volatile fields (created_at, approver timestamps) live in the non-hashed `locator` envelope (canon sec 10); semantic fields (scope, expiry, reason) are hashed content.

waiver_scope.description: What the waiver suppresses. At least one selector required. A waiver matches a finding iff every present selector matches.

finding_code.description: Restrict to one finding code (e.g. prohibition_unobservable).

desired_logical_id.description: Restrict to one desired entity (idmodel Tier-1 logical_id).

waiver_lifecycle.description: Lifecycle of the waiver record itself (distinct from idmodel's entity lifecycle).

title: waiver

waiver_id.description: Stable id = canon semantic-content digest over the hashed content fields {scope, reason, expiry, approver_ref, suppressed_states, target_revision?}. Identifies the waiver by its meaning.

reason.description: Why the drift is accepted. Required; an empty reason is invalid (no silent waivers).

expiry.description: Expiry as an ISO-8601 calendar DATE (YYYY-MM-DD), interpreted as end-of-day UTC. Required and bounded -- NO open-ended waivers in v1. After expiry the waiver lifecycle becomes `expired` and it stops suppressing. This is a semantic (hashed) field; it is a fixed date string, NOT a wall-clock timestamp, so it is deterministic across runs.

approver_ref.description: Reference to the human/governance approval (e.g. a downstream governance ratification id or governance_event_id). A REFERENCE, not a name/timestamp -- the wall-clock of approval is provenance and lives in locator.

suppressed_states.description: Which underlying result_states this waiver is permitted to suppress. MUST be a subset of [fail, unknown]. A waiver can NEVER suppress unsupported (that is a checker gap, not accepted drift) and never applies to pass/not_applicable.

target_revision.description: Optional: pin the waiver to one E(C)/spec revision digest. If present, the waiver only suppresses on that revision (forces re-approval on the next revision).

locator.description: NON-HASHED envelope (canon sec 10). Volatile/provenance only; changing these MUST NOT change waiver_id.
